info.name: Xwave Voice Agent API
info.description:
Complete Postman collection for testing the Voice Agent API.

Test order:
1. Health Check (verify all services)
2. Transcribe (test STT)
3. Analyze Sentiment (test NLP)
4. Speak (test TTS — save response as .mp3)
5. Chat (test the full agent)
6. Get Conversation (inspect history)

Import this file in Postman: File → Import → upload this JSON

request.description: Run this first! Verifies Ollama, Groq, HuggingFace, and LangSmith are all configured.

Upload an audio file and get the transcript back.

Tip: Record yourself on your phone, send to computer, test here.
Groq Whisper is incredibly fast — you'll see results in <1 second.

Test sentiment analysis with different texts.

Try:
- 'Thanks for the quick help!' → POSITIVE ~+0.99
- 'I need to reset my password' → mild positive
- 'This is terrible, nothing works!' → NEGATIVE ~-0.98

Note: First call may be slow (~20s) as HuggingFace loads the model.

url.raw: {{base_url}}/voice/speak?text=Hello, I am Xwave, your intelligent voice agent. How can I help you today?&voice=empathetic

query.value: Hello, I am Xwave, your intelligent voice agent. How can I help you today?

request.description:
Get audio back! In Postman:
1. Send the request
2. In the response area, click 'Save Response'
3. Save as response.mp3
4. Play the file!

Try different voices: default, male, empathetic, formal

exec: // Auto-save session_id from response for next requests

request.description:
Start a new conversation. The session_id is auto-saved to collection variables.

Then go to LangSmith (smith.langchain.com) and watch the trace appear in real time!
Continue the session with a frustrated message. Notice the agent's response tone shifts to be more empathetic.
Send enough negative messages to trigger escalation. Watch the action field change to 'escalate'.

View the full conversation history stored in SQLite.
Shows every turn with sentiment scores, model used, and escalation status.
